title: TerraPower Starts Building a New Nuclear Plant in Wyoming
summary: TerraPower, a company backed by Bill Gates, has received permission to start building its first nuclear power plant in Kemmerer, Wyoming. This is a big step because it's the first new nuclear construction approved in the US in almost ten years. The plant, called Natrium, is special because it uses liquid sodium for cooling instead of water, which makes it safer and more efficient. It also has energy storage, so it can save heat to make electricity later, helping it work well with renewable energy like solar and wind. The plant is smaller than most nuclear plants and is expected to be finished by 2030. Before it can operate, it still needs another license, but starting construction is an important milestone for clean energy.

background_read: Nuclear power plants make electricity without burning fossil fuels, which helps reduce pollution. TerraPower is trying to build a new type of plant that is safer and can store energy. This is important because the world needs more clean energy to fight climate change. The approval to start building is a big deal because it shows progress in developing new technologies for a greener future.
Article_Structure: WHO: TerraPower, a company backed by Bill Gates
WHAT: Building a new nuclear plant called Natrium with liquid sodium cooling and energy storage
WHY: To create a safer, more efficient nuclear plant that works well with renewable energy and helps provide clean electricity

perspectives.description: This plant could help reduce carbon emissions by providing clean energy.
It uses new designs like sodium cooling and energy storage to improve nuclear power.
